array.string: set -euo pipefail BACKUPS="$HOME/Library/GitLab-Data/data/backups" # find latest *_gitlab_backup.tar — GitLab's naming convention is # <timestamp>_<version>_gitlab_backup.tar LATEST=$(ls -1 "$BACKUPS"/*_gitlab_backup.tar 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -1) if [ -z "$LATEST" ]; then echo "$(date -Iseconds) NO BACKUP AVAILABLE in $BACKUPS — skipping" exit 0 fi # gitlab-backup restore expects the BACKUP= arg WITHOUT the trailing _gitlab_backup.tar TIMESTAMP=$(basename "$LATEST" | sed 's/_gitlab_backup\.tar$//') echo "$(date -Iseconds) restoring backup: $TIMESTAMP" docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-ctl stop unicorn 2>/dev/null || \ docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-ctl stop puma 2>/dev/null || true docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-ctl stop sidekiq 2>/dev/null || true docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-backup restore BACKUP="$TIMESTAMP" force=yes docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-ctl reconfigure docker exec gitlab-standby gitlab-ctl restart echo "$(date -Iseconds) restore complete"
